query: What is the refund policy for items purchased during an eBay Live stream?
response: For items purchased on eBay Live, the eBay Money Back Guarantee applies. If the item is not received, arrives damaged, or doesn't match the listing description, you can open a return request—even if the seller doesn't offer returns. However, if you return the item used or damaged, the seller may deduct an amount from your refund to cover lost value.

query: What are the rules for returning items I buy on eBay Live?
response:
For items purchased on eBay Live, the eBay Money Back Guarantee applies. If you don't receive the item, it arrives damaged, or it doesn’t match the listing description, you can open a return request—even if the seller doesn’t normally offer returns. 

If you return the item used or damaged, the seller may deduct an amount from your refund to cover the lost value. So you’re protected, but should return items in good condition to get a full refund.

query: If I win a bid during a seller’s stream but the item is later reported as counterfeit, what’s eBay Live’s policy on refunds?
response: If you win a bid on eBay Live and later find the item is counterfeit, you are protected by the eBay Money Back Guarantee. You can open a return request for an item that doesn't match the listing description, including counterfeit items, even if the seller doesn't offer returns. Once returned and verified, you would be eligible for a refund.

query: How do I place a bid during an eBay Live stream?
response: To place a bid during an eBay Live stream, first make sure you're signed into your eBay account registered in the US or Canada, with payment and shipping details saved. When an auction item is live, select the "Bid" button on that item. You can also choose "Max bid" to enter the highest amount you're willing to pay; eBay will automatically bid incrementally on your behalf up to that limit. You'll get an on-screen alert if you're outbid. Remember, all bids are final and cannot be retracted.

query: How do I participate in an eBay Live auction?
response:
To participate in an eBay Live auction, you need to:

1. Be signed into your eBay account registered in the US or Canada.
2. Have your payment and shipping details saved on your account.
3. Join the live stream event on eBay Live.
4. When an auction item is presented, click "Bid" to place your bid.
5. You can also select "Max bid" and enter the highest amount you're willing to pay; eBay will bid incrementally on your behalf up to that limit.
6. Watch for on-screen alerts if you are outbid and place higher bids if you want.
7. Each auction phase lasts at least 30 seconds, with the timer restarting if there are new bids, plus a soft close rule where bids in the last 5 seconds extend the auction by 5 more seconds.
8. Auctions usually last under 2 minutes per item.
9. All bids are final and cannot be retracted.

If you win, you will be automatically charged and notified in the app and via eBay inbox. You then have 4 days to complete payment. Let me know if you'd like details on anything specific!

query: How do I place a bid if I’m watching the live stream on my desktop but my phone has no internet?
response: To place a bid on eBay Live, you must be signed into your eBay account on the device you want to bid from and have an active internet connection. If your phone has no internet, you won’t be able to bid from it. However, since you are watching on your desktop, you can place bids there as long as your desktop has internet and you are signed in with your payment and shipping details saved. Just select "Bid" or "Max bid" on the item during the live stream on your desktop to participate.

query: Do I need to meet any requirements to participate in bidding on eBay Live?
response:
To participate in bidding on eBay Live, you need to:

- Be signed into an eBay account registered in the US or Canada (only buyers from these countries can participate)
- Have payment and shipping details saved on your account before bidding

If you meet these, you can join auctions and place bids during the livestream.

query: Do I need to meet any requirements to start bidding on eBay Live?
response:
Yes! To start bidding on eBay Live, you need to:

- Be signed into an eBay account registered in the US or Canada
- Have payment and shipping details saved on your account before bidding

Once those are set, you can participate in real-time auctions or buy fixed-price items during the livestream. Let me know if you want info on how bidding works!

query: Am I allowed to participate in an eBay Live auction if I'm located outside the US but have an eBay account?
response: eBay Live participation as a buyer is limited to users with eBay accounts registered in the US or Canada only. If your account is registered outside these countries, you won't be able to participate in eBay Live auctions.

query: Which product categories are available for bidding on eBay Live right now?
response: Currently, eBay Live offers bidding in these categories: Collectibles (sports trading cards, collectible toys, comics, coins & bullion, CCG, breaks, sports memorabilia), Luxury & Fashion (luxury watches, handbags, jewelry, apparel, sneakers, streetwear), and Toys. The categories continue to expand as the beta progresses.

query: What kinds of items can I buy during eBay Live streams?
response:
During eBay Live streams, you can buy items in these main categories:

- Collectibles: sports trading cards, collectible toys, comics, coins & bullion, collectible card games, breaks, sports memorabilia
- Luxury & Fashion: luxury watches, handbags, jewelry, apparel, sneakers, streetwear
- Toys

The available categories continue to expand as the beta progresses.

query: Are limited-edition digital art collectibles allowed to be purchased through eBay Live streams?
response: eBay Live currently supports categories like collectibles (sports cards, toys, comics, coins, etc.), luxury & fashion, and toys. Limited-edition digital art collectibles are not listed among the supported categories for purchase through eBay Live streams at this time. For the most up-to-date category info, you might check the eBay Live help pages.

query: I'm getting an error when I try to place a bid on eBay Live—how can I fix this?
response: If you’re getting an error placing a bid on eBay Live, first make sure you’re signed into an eBay account registered in the US or Canada with saved payment and shipping details. Without these, bidding won’t work. Also check your internet connection for stability. If all that’s set but you still get an error, try restarting the app or browser and clearing cache. If problems persist, you can visit ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ for troubleshooting, as specific error messages might require further support.

query: I'm having trouble placing a bid on eBay Live — what could be causing this?
response:
If you're having trouble placing a bid on eBay Live, here are some common reasons to check:

- Make sure you are signed into an eBay account registered in the US or Canada, as the platform is only available there.
- Confirm that your account has payment and shipping details saved; bidding requires this.
- Ensure the auction phase is active and not ended — auctions usually last under 2 minutes per item.
- If you already have the max bid set and it's outbid, you won't be able to place a higher bid unless you increase the max.
- Check your internet connection; a stable connection is needed for real-time bidding.
- Remember bids are final and you can't retract once placed.

If none of these apply, it might be a technical issue — try refreshing the page or restarting the app. If problems persist, you can visit ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ for more support.

query: I placed a bid but the stream froze right after—did my bid go through or do I need to try again?
response: If your bid was successfully submitted before the stream froze, it should have gone through. You would typically get an on-screen alert confirming your bid and notifying you if you're outbid. If you didn't receive a confirmation and the stream froze immediately, it's safer to try placing your bid again once the stream is working. Just be aware that all bids are final, so avoid placing duplicate bids for the same amount.

query: Does eBay Live support virtual reality shopping experiences?
response: eBay Live does not currently support virtual reality shopping experiences. It focuses on livestream video shopping through standard devices like smartphones and computers. For more details, you can visit ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ page.

query: Can I use eBay Live to buy items from other platforms too?
response: No, eBay Live only supports purchasing items that are listed and sold within the eBay Live platform itself. It doesn't support buying items from other platforms through the livestream experience. To buy on eBay Live, you need to watch and shop during the live events hosted on eBay Live.

query: Can I use eBay Live to make payments directly within the livestream chat?
response: On eBay Live, you can't make payments directly within the livestream chat itself. To participate in buying during a livestream, you need to be signed into your eBay account (registered in the US or Canada) with payment and shipping details saved. Then you place bids or buy items using the on-screen controls outside the chat. After winning an auction or buying a fixed-price item, the payment is automatically processed through eBay's system. If you want to pay for items, you do that through eBay's standard checkout flow, not directly in the chat.

query: What are eBay Live's policies on discounted pricing and promotions during my livestream?
response: eBay Live's provided information does not specify policies on discounted pricing or promotions during livestreams. Sellers can set starting prices and use auction or fixed-price formats, but details about running discounts or promotional campaigns during the stream aren't outlined. For specific guidance on promotions, you may want to visit ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ. Let me know if you'd like help with anything else!

query: What are the rules I need to follow when hosting a live stream on eBay Live?
response:
While eBay Live is currently invite-only for sellers, here are key rules and best practices you need to follow when hosting a live stream:

- Use only items from approved categories like collectibles, luxury & fashion, and toys
- Set starting prices at your minimum acceptable price to encourage bidding
- Use real-time auctions or fixed-price listings during the stream; reserve prices are supported
- Respond to buyer questions and show product details live to engage your audience
- Ship promptly after payment and always provide tracking numbers
- Respond to any payment disputes within 5 calendar days
- Have stable internet with 20+ Mbps upload, a good camera/mic setup, and test audio/video beforehand

For full eligibility, sellers must apply via the eBay Live Seller Interest Form and have an active eBay account in good standing plus relevant category expertise.

If you want details beyond this or official policies, please check ebay.com/help or the eBay Live FAQ.

query: Are there any specific restrictions for hosting live streams that feature crowdfunding or pre-order products on eBay Live?
response:
eBay Live's publicly available policies do not mention support or restrictions specifically related to crowdfunding or pre-order products in live streams. The platform focuses on real-time auctions and fixed-price sales of physical items in approved categories. 

For detailed guidance or clarification about hosting live streams with crowdfunding or pre-order items, I recommend checking eBay's official help pages or contacting eBay customer support directly.

query: How do I set up my first eBay Live stream to start selling?
response:
eBay Live is currently an invite-only beta for sellers. To set up your first stream, you need to apply by filling out the eBay Live Seller Interest Form. Eligibility requires an active, good-standing eBay account and demonstrated expertise in your category. Sellers with prior livestreaming experience and an established audience have better chances for approval.

Once invited:

- Prepare your setup with stable internet (20+ Mbps upload), smartphone or camera, tripod, good lighting, and a microphone.
- Test your audio and video before going live.
- Choose items with strong visual or collectible appeal.
- Create auction or fixed-price listings for the stream.
- Engage your audience by responding to comments and showcasing item details live.

If you want to get started, first submit the interest form at ebay.com/live and wait for approval. For more details, you can check the eBay Live Seller FAQs on the site.

query: How do I set up my first eBay Live stream?
response:
eBay Live is currently invite-only and in beta for sellers. To set up your first stream, you need to:

1. Fill out the eBay Live Seller Interest Form to apply.
2. Have an active eBay account in good standing with demonstrated expertise in your category.
3. Ideally have prior livestreaming experience and an established audience on other platforms to improve eligibility.
4. Prepare your setup: a smartphone or external camera, stable internet (20+ Mbps upload), good lighting, tripod, and microphone.
5. Test your audio and video before going live.
6. Once approved, you can schedule your livestream and select your selling format (auction or fixed-price).
7. Engage your audience, showcase products live, and promote your stream in advance.

If you haven't applied yet, start by submitting the interest form on eBay Live's site. For detailed steps after approval, you can check eBay Live Seller support resources once accepted.

query: How do I set up a live stream if I only have a pre-recorded video, not a live camera feed?
response: Currently, eBay Live requires a real-time live video feed for streaming. Using a pre-recorded video instead of live broadcasting is not supported on the platform. To host a live stream, you need to use a smartphone or external camera with a stable internet connection to broadcast in real time. If you want to learn more about technical setup, I can help!

query: What are the specific requirements I need to meet to start hosting a live stream on eBay Live?
response:
To start hosting a live stream on eBay Live, you need to:

1. Have an active eBay account in good standing.
2. Demonstrate expertise in your category through your transaction history.
3. Complete the eBay Live Seller Interest Form (it's invite-only and in beta).
4. Preferably have prior livestreaming experience and an established audience on other platforms, as eBay favors such applicants.
5. Ensure you have the technical setup: stable internet (20+ Mbps upload), smartphone or external camera, tripod, good lighting, external microphone, and ideally a second monitor for chat management.

Meeting these requirements is essential since eBay Live is currently invite-only and selectively invites sellers who meet these criteria.

query: Do I need to meet any specific requirements to start hosting a live stream on eBay Live?
response: Yes! To start hosting a live stream on eBay Live, you must be invited to the beta and fill out the eBay Live Seller Interest Form. You need an active eBay account in good standing and demonstrated expertise in your category, verified by your transaction history. eBay also prefers sellers with prior livestreaming experience and an established audience on other platforms. If you meet these criteria and get accepted, you can host live auctions or fixed-price sales during your broadcast.

query: I recently opened a new eBay store—can I start hosting eBay Live streams immediately, or is there a waiting period?
response: To host eBay Live streams, sellers must be invited and are currently required to fill out the eBay Live Seller Interest Form. Eligibility depends on having an active eBay account in good standing and demonstrated expertise in your category, often verified by your transaction history. Additionally, eBay favors sellers with prior livestreaming experience and an established audience. So, new store owners typically cannot start hosting immediately and need to go through the invitation and approval process.

query: Can I livestream and sell handmade jewelry on eBay Live?
response:
eBay Live is currently invite-only for sellers and in beta. To be eligible, you need an active eBay account in good standing and demonstrated expertise in your category verified by transaction history. eBay currently supports categories including collectibles, luxury & fashion (which includes jewelry), and toys.

Handmade jewelry would fall under the luxury & fashion category, so if you meet the eligibility and invitation criteria—especially with prior livestreaming experience and an established audience—you could potentially livestream and sell handmade jewelry on eBay Live.

If you're interested in selling on eBay Live, you should fill out the eBay Live Seller Interest Form and wait for an invitation.

query: Can I host a livestream if I sell products that don’t fit into the usual eBay categories?
response: eBay Live currently supports specific categories: Collectibles, Luxury & Fashion, and Toys. Since the beta is focused on these, sellers offering products outside these categories typically aren't eligible to host livestreams. If your products don't fit into these categories, you likely cannot host on eBay Live at this time. For more details and updates, consider checking the eBay Live Seller Interest Form or eBay's help resources.

query: Can I host a live sale for digital products like downloadable art on eBay Live?
response: eBay Live currently focuses on physical collectible and luxury/fashion items, toys, and expanding categories. Digital products like downloadable art are not supported for live sale on eBay Live at this time. For more details, you can check eBay's general policies or the eBay Live FAQ.

query: My livestream keeps disconnecting mid-broadcast; how can I fix this issue on eBay Live?
response: To reduce livestream disconnects on eBay Live, please ensure you have a stable internet connection with an upload speed of 20+ Mbps. Use a reliable smartphone or external camera, and test your audio and video before going live. Also, using good lighting and a tripod helps maintain stream quality. If the problem persists, try restarting your device and router, and avoid running other bandwidth-heavy applications during your broadcast.

query: My stream keeps having issues, how do I fix it?
response: For the best stream quality on eBay Live, make sure you have a stable internet connection with at least 20 Mbps upload speed. Use a good smartphone or external camera, along with a tripod, proper lighting, and an external microphone. Also, test your audio and video before going live to avoid issues. If problems persist, check your equipment and network setup. For detailed troubleshooting, you may want to visit the eBay Live Help or FAQ page.

query: My stream keeps disconnecting whenever I switch from my phone's Wi-Fi to mobile data—how can I fix this?
response:
Frequent disconnects when switching from Wi-Fi to mobile data are usually due to network interruptions. To improve stream stability on eBay Live, try these steps:

- Use a stable internet connection without switching during the stream.
- Test your phone's upload speed to ensure it’s 20+ Mbps as recommended.
- If you must switch networks, consider pausing or ending the stream first, then reconnect on the new network.
- Avoid using cell signal boosters or VPNs that might interfere with connectivity.

If issues persist, test streaming on a different network or device to isolate the problem. For more troubleshooting, check eBay Live help resources.

query: Can I use eBay Live to manage my entire eBay store inventory?
response:
eBay Live is designed for interactive livestream sales events rather than full inventory management. It lets you showcase and sell select items live via auctions or fixed-price listings, but it doesn't provide tools to manage your entire eBay store inventory.

For full inventory management, you should use eBay's seller tools on the main site or supported third-party software. eBay Live is best used as a complementary sales channel to promote and sell featured items in real time.

query: Can I use eBay Live to promote items outside of my eBay store?
response: On eBay Live, sellers can only promote and sell items that are listed on their eBay account. You cannot use eBay Live to promote items for sale outside of eBay. All transactions must be completed through eBay with the livestream integrated into the platform. If you want to sell on eBay Live, your items need to be part of your active eBay inventory.

query: Can I schedule a live stream to automatically start at midnight in a different time zone?
response: eBay Live does not currently support scheduling a live stream to automatically start at a specific time in a different time zone. You need to manually start the broadcast at your desired time. It’s best to plan your start time according to your local timezone and inform your audience accordingly. If you want more details on scheduling, you can check eBay Live resources or help pages.
